news.AI_Summary:
• TSMC will not deploy ASML's latest high-NA EUV lithography machines through 2029 to save costs, as they are 'very, very expensive' ($410 million apiece).
• TSMC is ASML's largest customer and will continue to benefit from current EUV technology.
• ASML expects high-NA EUV adoption for high-volume production in 2027-2028, targeting €60 billion revenue in 2030.
• ASML shares fell 3.3% on the news, despite a 31% gain year-to-date.

news.Title: Will Taiwan Semiconductor Be Worth More Than Nvidia by 2030?
news.Content: Taiwan Semiconductor (NYSE: TSM) has emerged as one of the most important players in artificial intelligence (AI). news.Title: Now Even Volvo Is Putting Tesla in the Rearview Mirror?
news.Content: Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) investors have been along for a bumpy ride over the last 18 months or so as they've seen competitors pull ahead of the automaker that helped reenergize the global electric vehicle (EV) industry. BYD , China's juggernaut EV maker, raced ahead of Tesla in global EV sales. And now, even Volvo is putting some distance between itself and Tesla, with Coca-Cola ...
news.AI_Summary:

• Tesla's volume semi production is set to begin in H1 2026 at its Nevada factory with a capacity of 50,000 trucks annually.

news.Title: Mark Zuckerberg sends shocking message to Meta employees
news.Content: Most tech companies talk abstractly about building AI from human behavior. Meta just sent its employees a memo making it concrete, and personal. The company is installing tracking software on employee computers. It will record mouse movements, keystrokes, clicks, and screenshots. And workers cannot opt out. The message behind the move is hard to misread. Meta is telling its own workforce that their daily behavior is now training data. What Meta is actually doing According to a memo sent to staff and seen by Reuters , Meta is rolling out a tool called the Model Capability Initiative. The software runs ...
news.AI_Summary:

• Meta stated the data is needed for AI agents to learn how people use computers and will not be used beyond AI training.

news.Title: Google Unveils 2 New AI Chips to Take on Nvidia
news.Content: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) made a name for itself by pioneering the graphics processing units (GPUs) that became the gold standard for rendering images in video games. The company adapted these chips to handle the rigors of artificial intelligence (AI) , a field it now dominates. Unfortunately, being the leader means there's always someone trying to take you down. Alphabet 's (NASDAQ: GOOGL) (NASDAQ: ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Google unveiled two new AI chips: the eighth-generation TPU 8t for training and TPU 8i for inference.
• The TPU 8t offers 3x compute performance, 10x faster storage access, and double the chip data transfer rate compared to its predecessor.
• The TPU 8i features high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and 3x SRAM, reducing latency for AI agents, and includes a custom Arm-based Axiom CPU.
• These innovations deliver 80% better performance-per-dollar, offering customers twice the volume for the same cost.
• Alphabet remains reliant on Nvidia's GPUs but aims to decrease reliance through its custom TPUs, offering more affordable AI processing options.

news.Title: What's Going on With Micron's Stock?
news.Content: Micron (NASDAQ: MU) investors have had some serious whiplash over the past few months. Earlier this year, the stock set a new all-time high, capping off a massive rally as investors dove headfirst into the stock to take advantage of the memory chip shortage. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron (MU) stock has experienced volatility, rallying to an all-time high, crashing 30%, and then returning to all-time highs within a month.
• The memory chip shortage is a key driver, with Micron estimating it can meet only half to two-thirds of HBM demand, causing prices to soar.
• The HBM market is projected to triple from $35 billion in 2025 to $100 billion in 2028.
• Google's TurboQuant algorithm, which reduces memory usage, could potentially alleviate the supply issue, but the overall crunch persists.
• Micron's stock has massive upside potential if the supply shortage continues for years, but could sell off heavily once resolved.
news.Keywords: stock volatility, memory chip shortage, HBM demand, AI, supply crunch, price surge, Google algorithm, upside potential
